questions.questionText: Which of the following sets of lengths can be used to construct a triangle?
options: 3 cm, 4 cm, 8 cm
2 cm, 5 cm, 7 cm
6 cm, 8 cm, 10 cm
4 cm, 5 cm, 10 cm
questions.explanation: A triangle can be constructed if and only if the sum of the lengths of any two sides is greater than the length of the third side. For 6, 8, and 10, we have 6+8 > 10. For the other options, this condition is not met (e.g., 3+4 < 8).

questions.questionText: The locus of points that are equidistant from two fixed points A and B is...
options: the angle bisector of ∠AOB, where O is the origin.
a circle with diameter AB.
the perpendicular bisector of the line segment AB.
a line parallel to AB.
questions.explanation: The definition of the perpendicular bisector of a segment AB is the set of all points that are equally distant from A and B.

questions.questionText: To construct an angle of 60° at point P on a line, what is the fundamental construction required?
options: Bisecting a right angle.
Constructing an equilateral triangle with P as one vertex.
Constructing a perpendicular bisector.
Drawing a circle centered at P.
questions.explanation: All angles in an equilateral triangle are 60°. By constructing an equilateral triangle with one vertex at P, a 60° angle is created.

questions.questionText: What is the locus of points inside an angle that are equidistant from the two lines that form the angle?
options: A circle.
A perpendicular line.
The angle bisector.
A parallel line.
questions.explanation: Any point on the bisector of an angle is equidistant from the two lines (arms) of the angle. This is a fundamental property of angle bisectors.

questions.questionText: You are asked to construct the tangent to a circle at a point P on its circumference. The first step is to draw the radius from the center O to P. What is the next step?
options: Bisect the angle ∠OPX for some point X.
Construct a line perpendicular to the radius OP at point P.
Draw a chord of any length from P.
Construct the perpendicular bisector of the radius OP.
questions.explanation: A tangent to a circle is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact. Therefore, the construction involves creating a 90° angle at point P with the radius.

questions.questionText: A triangle PQR has PQ = 7 cm, QR = 12 cm, and PR = 4 cm. Can this triangle be constructed?
options: Yes, because it is a scalene triangle.
No, because 7 + 4 is not greater than 12.
Yes, because all lengths are positive.
No, because the lengths are not in proportion.
questions.explanation: The triangle inequality states that the sum of the lengths of any two sides of a triangle must be greater than the length of the third side. Here, 7 + 4 = 11, which is less than 12. Therefore, the triangle cannot be constructed.

questions.questionText: The construction for bisecting an angle of 130° will result in two angles of:
questions.explanation: Bisecting an angle means dividing it into two equal angles. Therefore, 130° / 2 = 65°. The construction yields two angles of 65° each.

questions.questionText: To construct a line parallel to a given line AB through an external point P, one method involves drawing a transversal through P that intersects AB, and then...
options: bisecting the angle formed by the transversal.
constructing an equal corresponding angle at point P.
drawing a perpendicular from P to AB.
creating a circle with center P that touches line AB.
questions.explanation: If corresponding angles formed by a transversal intersecting two lines are equal, then the lines are parallel. This is the principle used for the construction.

questions.questionText: Two circles with equal radii are centered at A and B, and they intersect at points P and Q. What is the relationship between the line segment PQ and the line segment AB?
options: PQ is parallel to AB.
PQ has the same length as AB.
PQ is the perpendicular bisector of AB.
PQ is a tangent to both circles.
questions.explanation: Due to the symmetry of the construction, both A and B are equidistant from P and Q. This means AB is the perpendicular bisector of PQ. Conversely, both P and Q are equidistant from A and B, which means PQ is the perpendicular bisector of AB.

questions.questionText: A garden is to be planted. A tree (T) must be equidistant from two posts (P and Q) and also exactly 5 metres from a wall (W), which is a straight line. What two constructions are required?
options: An angle bisector and a circle.
A perpendicular bisector and a line parallel to the wall.
Two circles.
A line parallel to the wall and an angle bisector.
questions.explanation: The locus of points equidistant from two points (P and Q) is the perpendicular bisector of PQ. The locus of points 5 metres from a straight line (the wall) is a pair of parallel lines, one on each side. The intersection of these loci gives the possible positions for the tree.

questions.questionText: Which construction step is essential for creating a perpendicular to a line from a point on that line?
options: Drawing a single arc from the point.
Drawing two arcs from the point to cut the line on both sides.
Bisecting a 60° angle.
Constructing an equilateral triangle.
questions.explanation: To construct a perpendicular at a point P on a line, you first mark two points (A and B) on the line that are equidistant from P. Then you find the perpendicular bisector of the segment AB, which will pass through P and be perpendicular to the line.

questions.questionText: A treasure is buried in a field. It is equidistant from two intersecting straight paths. This means the treasure lies on...
options: a circle.
a perpendicular bisector.
the line parallel to one of the paths.
the pair of lines that bisect the angles between the paths.
questions.explanation: The locus of points equidistant from two intersecting straight lines is the pair of angle bisectors of the angles formed by the lines. These two bisectors are themselves perpendicular.
